core_mechanism: reconfigurable add-drop multiplexing of a single shared broadband resource into wavelength-addressed channels that are dynamically routed to chosen endpoint pairs, realizing arbitrary programmable connectivity graphs (full-mesh, partial-mesh, or partitioned sub-network slices) on demand
brief_reason: The abstract centers on a q-ROADM that spectrally slices one broadband source and dynamically distributes its channels to six users, programmatically reconfiguring full-mesh, partial-mesh, and sliced sub-network topologies according to link and service conditions.
